items.title: Phantom doubles down on perpetual futures with hire of Hyperliquid market builders
items.description: The crypto wallet is doubling down on perpetual futures after hiring the builders behind one of Hyperliquid's highest-profile market experiments.

items.title: Companies spending the most on AI are growing jobs, Ramp study finds
items.description: A new study from Ramp found companies making the biggest investments in AI are expanding their workforces, with heavy adopters increasing headcount by about 10% and entry-level hiring by 12%, challenging fears that generative AI is already causing widespread job losses.

items.title: Why OpenUSD's 'real threat' that tanked Circle stock still faces a steep uphill battle for adoption
items.description: The Stripe- and Coinbase-backed stablecoin consortium can challenge Circle's business model, but analysts say building a network is harder than assembling big-name partners.

items.title: SEC giving novel ETFs a rethink as it opens comment period on overhauling U.S. rules
items.description: Managers of exchange-traded funds, including those in the crypto sector, may see some changes at the Securities and Exchange Commission as it weighs its approach.

items.title: Chinese exile once linked to Trump strategist gets 30-year sentence in $1 billion fraud
items.description: The businessman known as Miles Guo, who pushed the fraudulent H-Coin project and had close ties to Steve Bannon, was finally sentenced after his 2024 conviction.

items.title: Jefferies warns of crypto market volatility as Clarity Act faces Senate test
items.description: The bank said the bill faces major hurdles, with passage boosting institutional crypto adoption and delays prolonging regulatory uncertainty.

items.title: Circle craters 17% as Stripe, Coinbase and BlackRock back rival stablecoin network
items.description: Open Standard's Open USD aims to let partners keep reserve income and eliminate minting fees, challenging Circle's USDC.

items.title: MetaMask launches Money Account with stablecoin yield and spending in one wallet
items.description: The launch reflects a broader push to make stablecoins more useful beyond trading and transfers.

items.title: Bitmine Ether buys eclipsed by $345M ETH ETF $345M outflows: Is sub $1.5K next?
items.description: Tokenization and RWA TVL growth highlight Ethereum’s fundamentals, but stagnant DApps and spot ETF outflows keep the pressure on ETH price.

items.title: SEC seeks public comment on regulating next generation of ETFs
items.description: The request from the US agency seeks feedback on how emerging ETF structures and investment strategies should be regulated as issuers roll out increasingly specialized products.

items.title: Financial companies join forces for US dollar stablecoin, keeping reserve earnings
items.description: The project, supported by Visa, Mastercard and many crypto companies, could be in a position to challenge Tether’s USDT and Circle’s USDC, currently the two largest stablecoins by market capitalization.

items.title: Nasdaq brings proprietary market data onchain through Pyth
items.description: The partnership extends distribution of Nasdaq's TotalView market data to blockchain applications and other software platforms through Pyth's marketplace.

items.title: Bitcoin price risks drop below $58K as US dollar hits 40-year high against yen
items.description: Bitcoin faced downside pressure as the US dollar hit its highest levels against the Japanese yen since 1986, while BTC price analysis revealed "capitulation" by 2025 top-buyers.

items.title: AI’s power crunch turns Bitcoin miners’ grid access into an asset
items.description: Bitcoin miners have the power sites AI companies need, but turning old mining campuses into real data center revenue is no easy pivot.

items.title: Crypto companies have spent $189M so far on 2026 US election cycle: Report
items.description: The crypto industry, big tech and gambling interest groups are betting big on politicians in the 2026 primaries and elections, with almost $300 million shelled out so far.

items.title: Business use of stablecoins set for growth surge: Cybrid report
items.description: The majority of businesses surveyed are likely to use stablecoins within the next 12 months, while regulatory clarity remained the biggest barrier to wider adoption.

items.title: Nike results top estimates even as China sales drop 12%; retailer expects $986 million tariff refund
items.description: Nike was expected to report another quarter of declining sales as the sneaker giant struggles to regain strength under its turnaround strategy.

items.title: Trump's annual financial disclosure shows more than $580M in crypto-related income
items.description: Trump's 2025 financial disclosure report totals more than 900 pages detailing holdings in cryptocurrency and stocks.

items.title: Record chip rally adds $2 trillion in combined value to Micron, Intel and AMD in second quarter
items.description: Wall Street poured into chipmakers not named Nvidia in the second quarter, as the artificial intelligence boom expanded to include more suppliers.

items.title: Michael Burry says he's shorting Caterpillar for the first time after it nearly doubled in the AI-driven rally of 2026
items.description: "Caterpillar jumped out at me," Burry said. "I have never shorted Caterpillar. It has always done great for me on the long side in the past."

items.title: Palo Alto, CrowdStrike both have best quarter ever as AI threats bolster cyber demand
items.description: The cybersecurity leaders are leaning into identity security, a critical cyber segment, as AI agents outnumber humans

items.title: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, blocks Trump order
items.description: President Donald Trump attended oral arguments in the case, underscoring his staunch opposition to granting automatic citizenship to many immigrants' babies.

items.title: Anthropic launches AI drug discovery program, joining tech giants in betting on healthcare
items.description: Anthropic will start an internal drug discovery program, part of a new push to sell artificial intelligence tools to drugmakers.

items.title: Big egg producers settle price inflation probe with DOJ for 53 million eggs — and $3.3M
items.description: Cal-Maine Foods, Versova and Hickman's Egg Ranch agreed to settle allegations of price manipulation by the Department of Justice and 17 states.

items.title: Bitcoin holds $58,131 support after 2.9% drop: Live levels
Bitcoin weak below $60k as rate jitters, ETF outflows persist
Crypto firms emerge as biggest corporate political spenders in 2026 U.S. midterms
Autheo Introduces the Internet Operating System: A Decentralized Coordination Layer for Web, Blockchain, & AI
Bitcoin ticks up above $60k, but heads for quarterly loss as ETF outflows persist
Strategy launches capital framework with $2.55B reserve and BTC sales plan
Solidion Technology plans to buy SpaceX shares as treasury asset
Bitcoin holds near 52-week low in bearish consolidation: Live levels

items.description: Mark Rutte makes economic case for Donald Trump to remain committed to the alliance in FT interview

items.title: Comcast’s ‘amicable divorce’ unwinds Hollywood’s corporate experiment
items.description: NBCUniversal split sparks talk of future deals amid ongoing media and entertainment consolidation

items.title: America’s stillborn 250th birthday
items.description: An event that was meant to celebrate an exceptional nation has been cornered by one man

items.title: How Elliott is becoming the hedge fund industry’s new ‘Tiger cub’ factory
items.description: The vaunted investor Paul Singer has spawned a small army of new firms mirroring Julian Robertson’s ‘Tiger cubs’

items.title: US Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to end birthright citizenship
items.description: Ruling is major blow to president’s immigration agenda

items.title: Supreme Court reminds America (and Trump) what citizenship is
items.description: The United States has always been based on the notion of welcoming newcomers from every country

items.title: World Bank to phase out China lending
items.description: Decision from global development institution follows years of pressure from the Trump administration

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wf5bKnlOpd0! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: The 20 best-performing stocks in the S&P 500 for the first half of 2026
items.description: The list is dominated by semiconductor and computer-hardware manufacturers.

items.description: These companies were hit hard as investors worried about eventual losses of market share to AI tools.

items.title: ‘I don’t think I’ll make it to 80’: I’m 70 and single. Do I take out a reverse mortgage or a home-equity agreement?
items.description: “I’m torn between these two options.”

items.title: ‘She wants him gone’: My friend took in a homeless man as a caretaker. After 10 years, how can she evict him?
items.description: “He lived rent-free, helping her with health-related issues and other tasks.”

items.title: Small-cap stocks just had their best start to a year since 1991. The rest of 2026 could look very different.
items.description: Small-cap stocks are coming off a historic run. On Tuesday, they clinched their best performance during the first six months of a year since 1991.

items.description: Nike’s profit and gross margins were buoyed by a tariff refund.

items.description: Before your single mom or dad remarries, talk with them about estate-planning issues.

items.title: ‘We are more likely to be living in poverty’: Social Security is a lifeline for women, so why are we ignored?

1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Witkoff and Kushner arrive in Doha for talks over US-Iran deal
items.description: Mediator Qatar says there are no plans for American delegation to meet Iranian officials

items.title: US stocks chalk up biggest quarterly gain in six years
items.description: Investors navigate Iran war fallout, chip stock volatility and blockbuster SpaceX IPO

items.title: Europe’s rearmament drive is sustaining 195,000 US defence jobs, Nato chief says
items.description: Mark Rutte makes economic case for Donald Trump to remain committed to the alliance in FT interview

items.title: ‘Unprecedented’ plane crash tests Beijing’s sense of security
items.description: Fatal flight of a light aircraft into Chinese capital’s tallest building raises questions about its defences

items.title: Space entrepreneurs borrow some surprisingly terrestrial tactics
items.description: Vertical integration helps to bridge the gap between today’s investments and returns that won’t come until later

items.title: Philip Morris allowed to market Zyn pouches in US as safer than smoking
items.description: Nicotine pouches are a focus of the tobacco industry’s push to grow beyond cigarettes

items.title: Key Burnham adviser pushes back at union call for bank tax rise
items.description: Trades Union Congress estimates restoring previous surcharge could raise £9bn

items.title: Egg producers settle US claims they manipulated benchmark prices
items.description: Federal and state authorities cite three-year effort to co-ordinate bids that inflated quotations

items.title: Neon Buys ‘Artificial,’ a Film About OpenAI, After Amazon Dropped It
items.description: Neon purchased “Artificial,” which focuses on OpenAI’s chief, Sam Altman, after Amazon walked away from it following an investment in the start-up.

items.title: Court Halts Pentagon Rule Requiring Escorts for Journalists
items.description: A federal judge temporarily blocked a new rule that restricted reporters’ access to the Pentagon, in a lawsuit brought by The New York Times.

items.title: Comcast, NBCUniversal Split Drives Deal-Making Speculation
items.description: Almost as soon as the two companies announced a split, analysts rushed to consider potential merger and acquisition targets for each.

items.title: NPR Retracts Article That Mistakenly Said Justice Alito Would Retire
items.description: The news outlet quickly removed the article, which was written by NPR’s seasoned Supreme Court reporter, Nina Totenberg, from its website.

items.title: BMW Will Build a New Electric S.U.V. in South Carolina
items.description: The German company is doubling down on electric vehicles even as other automakers pull back after acknowledging billions of dollars in losses.

items.title: Nursing Students and Others Get Higher Student Loan Limits
items.description: A legal challenge to a law that takes effect this week has doubled the amount certain graduate students can borrow from the federal government to $50,000 per year.

items.title: How the Supreme Court Ushered in Corporate Chaos in D.C.
items.description: Though the high court affirmed the political independence of the Federal Reserve, it gave the presidency huge control over dozens of federal regulators.

items.title: Prominent Tech Founder in Indonesia Is Found Guilty in Corruption Case
items.description: The case against Nadiem Makarim, a co-founder of Gojek, has fueled concerns about judicial fairness in a nation where foreign investors were already growing wary.
